_note: Two-plane decomposition for TKT-0012 / ADR-0125 — shipping ui-command-modal, a Pattern-class UIElement COORDINATOR that nests a ui-modal for the surface (ADR-0017/0019/0020) and re-derives ui-combo-box's active-descendant filter (ADR-0085) over author [role=option]/[role=group] children (ADR-0017 child-move). OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the component + its catalog exclusion + its site pages touch (controls/, a2ui allowlist, site). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the shipped control must DO. Crossed via hosts. plan:true — each leaf carries the checkable acceptance predicate the builder's tests must satisfy; no source exists yet under controls/command-modal/. COMPOSITION-heavy by design: ui-modal is nested (not re-implemented), the combo-box active-descendant pattern is re-derived (private methods, ~60 lines), scroll-fade + ADR-0017 child-move reused — NO new base class, trait, event, or geometry row (no novelty leg). LLD-C16 (the CommandModal EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry) is an a2ui-package slice dispatched to a2ui-builder after the descriptor ships.

nodes.label: ui-command-modal (the shipped Pattern-class palette)
tag + identity
tag ui-command-modal, class UICommandModalElement, folder controls/command-modal/, extends UIElement, tier pattern, self-define
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: `customElements.get('ui-command-modal')` resolves; the instance is `instanceof UIElement` and NOT `instanceof UIFormElement` (face.formAssociated:false — holds no form value); `controls/command-modal/command-modal.ts` exists and self-defines on import; the descriptor resolves `tier: pattern` (SPEC-R1)

nodes.label: props schema (open/label/placeholder/hotkey)
open prop — reflected boolean default false, bindable two-way (drives the nested modal)
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: open reads false unset; `el.open=true` reflects `open=""` and back; setting open drives the nested ui-modal's open (SPEC-R2 AC1/AC2, SPEC-R3 AC2)

nodes.label: label prop — string default '' (search field aria-label reactive + dialog name forwarded to the nested modal at connect)
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: label reads '' unset, round-trips attribute↔property; a non-empty label sets the search field's aria-label (reactive) AND is forwarded onto the nested ui-modal at part creation; '' falls back to the default search name (SPEC-R2, SPEC-R3 AC3, SPEC-R4 AC3)

nodes.label: placeholder prop — string default '' (search field placeholder via the [data-empty]::before read-back)
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: placeholder reads '' unset, round-trips; the search field's data-placeholder attribute follows it reactively (SPEC-R2)

nodes.label: hotkey prop — string default '' (the F2 opt-in; '' = no listener)
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: hotkey reads '' unset, round-trips attribute↔property; a grep of static props confirms NO persistent/value/recents/results/size prop exists (SPEC-R2 AC3)

nodes.label: nested ui-modal surface seam — open→modal, sync the modal's close/toggle back + re-emit, forward the name, NO own dialog/backdrop/Escape
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: a nested ui-modal exists in the palette light DOM; `el.open=true` opens it; a simulated modal `close` syncs `el.open=false` and re-emits close+toggle exactly once (open is the discriminator — a palette-driven close does NOT double-fire); a grep of command-modal.ts finds NO createElement('dialog'), no showModal, no backdrop-rect handler, no cancel handler — all delegated to ui-modal (SPEC-R3 AC1/AC2)

nodes.label: search field part — role=combobox contenteditable, aria-controls/expanded/autocomplete, focus-on-open, reactive aria-label
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` (jsdom) + `npm run test:browser command-modal` — the search part has role=combobox, aria-autocomplete=list, aria-controls→the list id, is contenteditable=plaintext-only with autocorrect/autocapitalize/autocomplete/spellcheck OFF; the host carries NO role/aria-*; on open, DOM focus lands in the search field (browser leg — jsdom focus is unreliable) (SPEC-R4)

nodes.label: filter — hidden-based substring/keyword match, empty-group-heading hide, active reset
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: typing a query hides options whose textContent+data-keywords do not case-insensitively include it; a group with zero surviving options hides its heading; the query change resets the active option (no stale aria-activedescendant) (SPEC-R5 AC1/AC3)

nodes.label: active-descendant navigation — #getVisibleOptions/#setActive/#moveActive, aria-activedescendant + [data-active], focus STAYS in the search field
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser command-modal` (Chromium+WebKit) — with focus in the search field, ArrowDown advances aria-activedescendant to the next visible enabled option and moves [data-active] while document.activeElement STAYS the search field; ArrowUp reverses (wrapping); Home/End jump first/last; disabled+hidden options are skipped (SPEC-R5 AC2 — browser-only: jsdom locators are blind to real focus retention)

nodes.label: selection — click/Enter commit → emit select {value,label,group} + close
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: Enter on the active option (or a click on an enabled option) fires exactly one `select` with detail {value,label,group} matching the option, then sets open=false (the modal closes); a click on a disabled/aria-disabled option emits nothing (SPEC-R6 AC1)

nodes.label: Escape is the modal's single path — NO palette Escape branch, NO clear-first stage
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser command-modal` — with the palette open and a filter active, Escape closes it via the nested modal's single path (one close emission), NOT a two-stage clear-then-close; a grep of command-modal.ts finds NO `key === 'Escape'` branch (SPEC-R9)

nodes.label: result-count live region — [data-part=status] aria-live=polite, updated on filter
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: a visually-hidden [data-part=status] aria-live=polite region exists; after a filter leaving N visible options its text announces `${N} results` (and `No results` at zero); the region is present in the a11y tree but visually hidden in CSS (SPEC-R7)

nodes.label: empty state — author [slot=empty] override else a default role=presentation No-results row
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: a query matching nothing with no author slot shows the default 'No results' row (role=presentation — not reachable by Arrow/Enter/click); an author [slot=empty] child shows INSTEAD of the default row (SPEC-R8 AC1)

nodes.label: opt-in hotkey binding — a teardown-returning effect binds ONE document keydown ONLY while hotkey is non-empty (per-instance, no singleton)
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — jsdom: with hotkey='' a spy on document.addEventListener sees NO keydown for this instance; with hotkey='mod+k' a ⌘/Ctrl+K document keydown is preventDefault'd and toggles open; disconnect removes the listener; a hotkey change aborts the old and installs the new (no residue/duplicate) — via the effect's returned teardown, NOT an always-on gated listener (SPEC-R10 AC1/AC2)

nodes.label: negative controls — NO @agent-ui/router import, NO command bus, no off-allowlist event
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — a grep/import-scan of the built module finds NO import of `@agent-ui/router` and no command/action registry; no event outside change·input·select·open·close·toggle is ever dispatched from the host (SPEC-R6 AC2) — the DAG + not-a-bus law (ADR-0115, TKT-0012)

nodes.label: command-modal.css — search/list/item/group/active geometry, bounded scroll viewport + scroll-fade, container surface via the nested modal, --ui-command-modal-* token chain, forced-colors
nodes.accept: `npm test` (family-coherence.test.ts) + `npm run test:browser command-modal` — the single command-modal.css scopes to :where(ui-command-modal), declares the --ui-command-modal-* chain (search-block-size off the entry-control register, list-max-block, item pad/gap, active-bg), the list is a bounded scroll viewport (max-block-size + overflow-y:auto, scroll-fade wired), and a @media (forced-colors: active) block keeps the [data-active] active-option highlight legible; the aria-activedescendant/[data-active] pairing is the non-color signifier (no color-only intent); fleet invariants green (SPEC-R11)

nodes.label: command-modal.md descriptor — tier:pattern, extends UIElement, events(select/open/close/toggle), parts(search/list/status/empty), content model, keyboard map, composes:[ui-modal]
nodes.accept: `npm test` (family-coherence.test.ts A-group) — the descriptor exists, tag/class/folder aligned, tier:pattern, extends UIElement (in BASE_CLASSES), face.formAssociated:false; events all ⊂ the allowlist with NO `dismiss`; the parts, the [role=option]/[role=group] content model, the [slot=empty] slot, and the keyboard map (Arrow active-descendant, Enter select, Escape=modal-owned) are all declared (SPEC-R12 AC2)

nodes.label: descriptor ↔ static props trip-wire
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` — the attributes[] fence in command-modal.md mirrors static props 1:1 (open/label/placeholder/hotkey) via compareDescriptorToProps (red on any drift); compareDescriptorToSource confirms no undocumented styled part/role/custom-state (SPEC-R12 AC1)

nodes.label: catalog disposition — PERMANENT EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST CommandModal (a2ui build slice)
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a2ui index.test.ts) — the moment command-modal.md ships, `CommandModal` enters FLEET_TYPES (ADR-0087); the coverage gate stays green because CommandModal is a permanent EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry (with the ADR-0112 cl.6 reason), NOT a catalog row; the residue-guard confirms CommandModal is absent from every catalog/factory key (SPEC-R13 AC1)

nodes.label: jsdom probes — props reflect/fail-open, parts/ARIA, filter, selection detail, no-router negative control, hotkey add/remove, reconnect residue
nodes.accept: `npm test command-modal` green — reflection round-trips + fail-open for the props; parts+ARIA via parts (not the host); the filter/empty-group/status legs; the select {value,label,group} detail; the no-router/no-off-allowlist-event negative control; the hotkey add-only-when-set + remove-on-disconnect spy; a disconnect/reconnect probe shows no duplicate listeners

nodes.label: cross-engine browser probes — real typeahead-filter + keyboard flow (focus stays in the search field), whole-shape opened dialog, Escape-via-modal single path
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser command-modal` (Chromium+WebKit) — type filters the list; ArrowDown/Up move aria-activedescendant with document.activeElement STAYING the search field; Enter emits select+closes; Escape closes via the modal's single path; the whole-shape leg proves the palette opens as a real centered dialog (top-layer) with a populated, grouped, scrollable list (not a collapsed panel). jsdom-green ≠ done — focus retention + the <dialog> top-layer are browser-only (the combo-box/date-time precedent)

nodes.label: barrel/exports/size integration (ADR-0080 marginal)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (barrels.test.ts, exports drift gate); `npm run size` run by hand (ADR-0040 §3) — the command-modal leave-one-out marginal measured and pinned if material (note ui-modal is already in the family total; the marginal is the palette's own delta)

nodes.label: site pages {doc,demo} + representative gallery specimen
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts + gallery/preview suites) — command-modal-doc.html (descriptor-derived API page) and command-modal-demo.html exist, satisfying the tier:pattern required {doc,demo} set; the demo renders an OPENED palette over a realistic backdrop with ≥2 groups + multiple real command items (icon+label+shortcut) + the empty-state affordance + a keyboard-flow callout + both hotkey modes; the preview specimen is a POPULATED grouped command list, not a one-child stub; knobs surface label/placeholder/hotkey/open one-per-prop (SPEC-R14)

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO before commit
nodes.accept: a fresh-context `component-reviewer` pass returns GO against the descriptor + anatomy + composition + contract fidelity (the standing per-control-wave DoD); generator ≠ critic

actions.label: identify itself under the fleet ui-* tag law as a Pattern-class UIElement coordinator in controls/, self-defining, holding no form value
carry a reflected bindable open prop that is the single drive signal for its nested modal surface
carry a label that names both the search combobox (reactively) and the dialog (forwarded at connect)
carry a placeholder for its search field
carry an opt-in hotkey string and own NO persistent/value/recents/results/size prop
render its surface by nesting a ui-modal, driving and syncing its open, and owning no dialog/backdrop/Escape machinery of its own
present a role=combobox search field that keeps focus and takes focus on open
filter its option list on type by case-insensitive substring/keyword match, hiding empty group headings
move a highlighted active-descendant option with the arrow keys without ever moving DOM focus off the search field
emit select {value,label,group} and close when a command is chosen
delegate Escape/dismissal entirely to the nested modal as a single close path
announce its visible result count through an aria-live region
show an empty-state affordance (author slot or default row) when nothing matches
bind at most one per-instance document keydown, only while its hotkey is set, with no global singleton
import no router and run no command bus — navigation is the consumer's select handler
lay out its search/list/item/group/active parts to the Pattern-class geometry with a bounded scroll viewport and stay legible under forced-colors
record its public surface truthfully in a tier:pattern descriptor
keep its descriptor in 1:1 bijection with its live props
stay permanently out of the generative-UI catalog as app-owner launcher chrome
prove props/parts/ARIA/filter/selection/no-router/hotkey under jsdom
prove real focus-retaining keyboard flow and the whole-shape opened dialog cross-engine
stay within the family size budget, exported through the public barrel
give the fleet an API doc page, an opened-palette demo, and a representative specimen
pass independent review before shipping

the search field's input event drives the filter

Enter commits the active option the navigation tracks

the empty-state row is toggled from the zero-visible outcome of the filter
the hotkey binding effect reads the hotkey prop and installs/removes the listener
the no-router/no-bus negative control fences exactly what the commit path must NOT do
the CSS styles the search/list/item/group/active parts the connected() wiring creates
the [data-active] active-option highlight + forced-colors legibility live in the CSS
the descriptor's attributes[] fence + parts + events describe the class's live surface

the descriptor's events[] mirror what the commit/dismiss paths emit

the jsdom probes assert prop reflection + the parts/ARIA/filter/selection/hotkey behavior
the hotkey add/remove spy is a jsdom probe
the no-router/no-event negative control is a jsdom probe

the opened <dialog> top-layer whole-shape proof needs a real engine (jsdom has no top layer)
the Escape-via-modal single-path proof runs cross-engine
the whole-shape populated/scrollable list proof needs a real layout engine
the barrel export + size marginal target the shipped control
the doc page derives from the descriptor; the demo needs the shipped element
the demo's opened-palette surface exercises the nested modal
the site pages ship alongside the descriptor+exclusion wave
review runs once the gates it grades against are green
